content:
Welcome back, folks. This will be a brief session today.
- Review the risk disclaimer before starting.
- We will cover the Dollar Index (DXY), Euro Dollar, and E-mini S&P.
- It is highly recommended to watch the previous live stream (available on the 2023 Internship playlist on
YouTube, prior to this video) for context, to avoid the appearance of 'cherry picking'.

- BEARISH ORDER BLOCK & INVERSION GAP
On the weekly chart of the Dollar Index:
- Last night, I mentioned wanting to see price draw up into the low and open, specifically of this 'polish
candle', which is identified as a Bearish Order Block on the weekly chart.
- This expectation was delivered handsomely today.
- The shaded area, representing a very specific gap between this candle's low and another candle's high,
was treated as an Inversion Gap or Inversion Fair Value Gap, expected to act as support. A closer look will
be taken on lower timeframes.

- IMMEDIATE REBALANCE AND MARKET BIAS
On the daily chart, the same weekly Bearish Order Block's opening price is extended.
- For Wednesday, May 24, 2023, price opened and dropped into Monday's high.
- The shaded area (inversion fair value gap) acted as support, trading specifically to Monday's high.
- This action is termed an 'immediate rebalance'. It is very powerful if you have a clear understanding of the
'draw on liquidity' (where the market is likely to reach for stops or inefficiency for rebalancing/repricing).
- With our shared bias from last night (targeting this level), the DXY's performance was seen, subsequently
lending to weakness in Euro and other foreign currencies.
- The target (weekly Bearish Order Block opening price) has been met. Due to personal circumstances,
clarity for future expectations is limited, so the focus is on reviewing today's actions.

- BEARISH BIAS & IMMEDIATE REBALANCE
On the Euro Dollar:
- The weekly chart indicated a likely draw towards a specific order block, and some movement towards this
occurred today, despite a 'wonky' session.
- On the daily chart, we observe a mirror image of the Dollar Index's action.
- Our bias was bearish for Euro (and bullish for Dollar). Today, Euro opened and traded up to Monday's low,
which acted as an 'immediate rebalance', before price started to go lower.
- Relative equal lows and a Fair Value Gap in the weekly bullish order block are noted. The belief remains
in a downside draw for Euro, which can still occur even if the Dollar Index trades in consolidation, seeking
further weakness on the downside. No bullish signs are present for Euro.

- RETAIL RESISTANCE AND SELL SIDE TARGETS
On the hourly chart for Euro Dollar:
- Monday's low is shown as the 'immediate rebalance'. Intraday, 'relative equal highs' were observed
(treated as retail resistance).
- The sell side liquidity pool was targeted, as outlined in last night's livestream, confirming a downside draw.
- Price rallied up, engaged buy side liquidity, and then the Monday immediate rebalance on Monday's low
was re-engaged. Displacement to the downside followed.
- Currently, relative equal lows are again present, framing the 1.07 big figure level as a continued downside
draw.
- Watch for inefficiency (sell side imbalance, buy side inefficiency) and relative equal highs, where price
could potentially 'stab up' before resuming lower, possibly into the Asia and London sessions.

- AM SESSION ICT SILVER BULLET TRADE
On the 5-minute chart for Euro Dollar, going into the AM session:
- The market ran up into Monday's low (the 'immediate rebalance'), tapped it again, and then broke a swing
low, indicating a 'shift in market structure'.
- The ICT Silver Bullet trade is looked for during the 10 AM to 11 AM New York local time window.
- Entry Options:
- A Fair Value Gap formed prior to 10 AM.
- A Fair Value Gap formed inside the 10 AM to 11 AM hour window.
- Both offered valid short entries. The 'rejection block' (the lowest closing price of down-closed candles, as
taught in the PDRA Matrix in core content) was identified at 1.07506.
- Entries were in the 72s or 74s, offering more than 15 pips to the rejection block, validating both AM
session ICT Silver Bullet short trades. This serves as another example of the Silver Bullet working in Forex.

- BEARISH OUTLOOK & HOLIDAY CONSIDERATIONS
On the E-mini S&P daily chart:
- The previous stance was bullish until the 'mean threshold' of the order block and a specific Fair Value Gap
were lost.
- If these support levels were lost, the market was expected to go down and clear 'sell side liquidity'.
- Current Context: Approaching a holiday weekend. Mentions 'tenfold hat discussions' and Senators being
offered satellite phones, hinting at potential urgency for price to move lower and 'sell that narrative' (a fear
narrative).
- Relative equal lows are present, suggesting remaining sell side liquidity. It's likely tonight will involve
closing in the 'opening range gap'.

- INVERSION FVG & SELL SIDE RAID
On the hourly chart for E-mini S&P:
- The lows where 'sell side liquidity' would be rated were identified in the previous video.
- If the mean threshold and the Fair Value Gap (up here) did not offer support, they would be treated as an
'inversion fair value gap'.
- Price did go up, offered 'consequent encumbrance' (consequent rebalance), and then sold off, attacking
the sell side liquidity pool.
- Notice the candle bodies stopping right at that low, suggesting price went for that liquidity and then came
back up into a 'short-term premium'.

- NEWS IMPACT AND SELL STOP CLEARANCE
On the 15-minute Candlestick chart for E-mini S&P:
- In the morning, prior to 8:30 AM news, a buy side liquidity pool was present. A sell side imbalance/buy
side inefficiency (Fair Value Gap) formed, into which price hit, followed by displacement to the downside,
clearing all sell stops resting below the 4120 level.
- In the afternoon's final hour of trading, a 'shift in market structure' occurred. Price came back down into a
bullish Fair Value Gap (buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency) and an Order Block.
- Subsequently, price rallied back up into this Fair Value Gap, specifically targeting the 'opening range gap'.

- OPENING RANGE GAP AND STRONG NARRATIVE
On the 5-minute chart for Futures trading:
- The 'opening range gap' is the separation between Tuesday's close and Wednesday's open, visible by
using the 'regular trading hours' (RTH) tab on TradingView.
- This gap is typically expected to fill. However, when there is a strong narrative in play (e.g., Dollar higher
to its weekly bearish order block, Euro weaker, sell models in equities), the market will usually go for its
objective (the 4120 level, where sell stops were located).
- While it's generally advisable to avoid shorting when such a gap exists, a strong narrative can override
this. Price opened, rolled right down into the objective, and consolidated around it until noon.

- AM SILVER BULLET & DEEP ORDER FLOW ANALYSIS
On the 1-minute chart for E-mini S&P:
- An up-close candle, identified as a bearish order block, formed after a 'shift and change in the state of
delivery'. The market traded back up into this during the 10-11 o'clock window.
- Inefficiencies and Lower Timeframes: For situations where a fair value gap isn't immediately obvious on a
1-minute chart, one must consult lower timeframes (intraday matrix: 45s, 30s, 15s, 5s, 1s charts).
- Strong Bias: If there's a strong bias (like the 4120 target, DXY reaching its weekly bearish order block),
these lower timeframes allow for an inspection of 'real order flow'. The bearish order block is not just any upclose candle; it's significant within the narrative of drawing to liquidity below 4120 and drawing up into a
short-term premium, where algorithms short.

- ORDER BLOCK INEFFICIENCY & SILVER BULLET
VALIDATION
On the 15-second chart, examining the same order block:
- Inside that order block, inspecting the price action reveals inefficiencies, specifically a Fair Value Gap.
- It's crucial to understand that simply trading into any random up-close candle doesn't make it an ICT
bearish order block. There must be a narrative indicating a draw down into liquidity (e.g., below 4120) and a
draw up into a short-term premium.
- This is where 'smart money' operates: when price rallies up into this inefficiency (Fair Value Gap), they go
short, and the market spools lower, attacking the sell side.
- This confirms the AM ICT Silver Bullet trade, emphasizing that one must go beneath the 1-minute
timeframe to dissect 'real order flow' with an 'x-ray view' when a narrative for a predetermined level exists.

- TRADE CRITERIA AND PROFITABILITY
Regarding the viability of the E-mini S&P Silver Bullet trade:
- If the stops are at 41.20, it is not unreasonable to expect price to go three handles below that (e.g.,
4117.75) to raid liquidity.
- Assuming an entry at 4127.75 (from the inefficiency observed earlier):
- Minimum Criteria for a High Probability ICT Silver Bullet Trade: It must offer 10 handles.
- This trade met the criteria, offering a 10-handle range in the move. The trade delivered shortly after 11
o'clock.
- Access to platforms that offer these granular timeframes (like TradingView) is essential for seeing and
executing such trades. This method works, allowing for consistent profitability even on 'ugly, choppy, messy
days', by providing a way to dissect real order flow.

section_title: E-MINI S&P POST-NOON REVERSAL AND OPENING RANGE GAP ANALYSIS
content:
After the initial downside movement:
- The first pass below the 4120 level occurred during the Silver Bullet trade. Price then traded lower to
4117.75, which became the low of the day, occurring around noon.
- Confluence at Noon: This low coincided with the Dollar Index going right up into its weekly bearish order
block opening price. This sequence (hitting target, raiding liquidity below stops) marked the end of the day's
downside objective.
- Post-noon, price consolidated until the last hour of the day (3 PM to 4 PM New York session), during
which Index Futures worked towards filling the 'opening range gap' that had not been worked inside all day.
- A 'shift in market structure' was observed after 12 o'clock (a typical 'lunch reversal'), with displacement to
the upside, forming a bullish order block (three consecutive down-closed candles) and a Fair Value Gap.

section_title: E-MINI S&P OPENING RANGE GAP FILL EXPECTATION
content:
Considering the current price action (at 6:05 PM New York time):
- The shaded area represents the 'opening range gap' on the regular trading hours chart.
- The expectation is to see price trade up into 41.58, close that gap in, and then assess if it's willing to go
any higher, potentially bumping into another small gap.
- More specifically, the desire is to see this entire gap filled in.
- Future Scenario: If price trades up, hits that target, closes the gap, and then starts to fall again, the Dollar
would simultaneously need to be going higher. If that's the case, then the 'relative equal lows' on the S&P
would be targeted and taken out.
CONCLUSION
- VALIDATION AND LEARNING PATIENCE
In conclusion:
- The 'cherry-picked' future daily and weekly candles delivered exactly as outlined last night, demonstrating
that this methodology works.
- This system performs consistently when called and outlined.
- Patience in Learning: It is crucial to prevent impatience from wanting to make money immediately.
Learning patience comes from focusing on learning, understanding, and growth, rather than solely on
monetary gain.
- Mastering the identification of 'draws on liquidity' does not happen overnight. Nothing can accelerate this
process. Consistent daily engagement with price action reviews is the only way to grow your understanding
between now and November.
